title: Ruilopezia bracteosa
intro: Ruilopezia bracteosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, Ruilopezia bracteosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ruilopezia bracteosa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ruilopezia bracteosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Ruilopezia bracteosa is classified within the family Cistaceae, commonly known as the rockrose family. This family consists of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Ruilopezia bracteosa is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaves, flowers, and growth habits.
